behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing, and all missing required fields are listed in one clear error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (validator must gate downstream pipeline calls). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed. Rejected Unit because behavior text says "before any downstream processing," which requires wiring-level verification that downstream steps are not invoked.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake service with missing `name` and `insurance_id` using stubbed downstream services; assert returned validation error lists both fields and assert insurance/triage/scheduling clients were not called.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: pure validation logic in one function/object boundary. No module handoff, real infra, or model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Call DOB validator directly with representative invalid inputs and assert specific validation failures for future, >150-year-old, and malformed date values.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code and maps payer statuses `eligible`/`not eligible`/`pending`.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is adapter wiring + status mapping across modules (service -> payer client -> mapper). Rejected System: although payer is external, this behavior text is about payload fields and status interpretation, which a deterministic stub API can expose without real network faults.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub payer endpoint/client to capture outbound request; assert patient ID and plan code fields. Return each status variant and assert internal eligibility state mapping.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is set to `pending` and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Failure is fallback orchestration between payer client error handling and intake state progression. Rejected System: behavior text targets business fallback ("marks ... pending and continues") rather than transport-specific network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure payer client stub to throw timeout/connection errors; run intake and assert eligibility becomes `pending` and subsequent triage/scheduling steps still execute.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: Tests request construction across config + symptom input + model client boundary. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is only about model input payload inclusion, not correctness/quality of model output.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke triage service with known symptoms and rubric config against a stub model client that records request payload; assert both symptom content and rubric are present and correctly formatted.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model judgment/classification quality; a stub would always pass and hide failures. Not Workflow because this behavior is one model decision, not multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom fixtures and assert high-urgency classification rate meets threshold; track variance to catch regression in model behavior.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser persists a clinician-readable explanation field, not just numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at parser-to-storage handoff (data mapping + persistence). Rejected Agentic because behavior text checks stored field presence/shape, not whether explanation quality is clinically good.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response fixture containing score + explanation through parser/storage path and assert persisted record includes non-empty clinician-readable explanation column/field.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale rather than generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is output quality/content specificity from a real model; deterministic mocks hide the failure. Not Workflow because it evaluates one live model output step.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on multiple symptom cases; assert explanation references case symptoms and urgency rationale with rubric-based checks, and fails generic-template responses.

behaviors.description: Scheduling assigns first available slot matching urgency SLA (high: same-day, medium: within 48h).
behaviors.justification: Needs module interaction between urgency policy, availability query, and calendar write. Rejected Unit because behavior requires end-to-end scheduling decision over shared calendar state, not just isolated sorting logic.
behaviors.test_description: Seed test calendar DB with controlled availability; run scheduler for high/medium urgency and assert chosen slot is earliest slot satisfying each SLA window.

behaviors.description: Concurrent intake processing does not double-book the same slot.
behaviors.justification: Critical failure is concurrency/transaction behavior at application+DB boundary; local integration with real test DB transaction semantics is needed. Rejected System because no external OS/network/subprocess dependency is required beyond local DB concurrency control.
behaviors.test_description: Start two concurrent scheduling requests targeting the same remaining slot against transactional test DB; assert exactly one booking succeeds and the other is retried/fails cleanly without duplicate row.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes required appointment details and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Primarily deterministic formatting/composition rules in one boundary; no required external infra or model judgment. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require cross-module orchestration, only message content correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation message builder with fixture appointment/patient/provider/visit-type data and assert message contains name, time, provider, and correct prep instructions for each visit type.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: This is multi-module orchestration/state handoff, but explicitly with deterministic stubbed triage output. Rejected Agentic due behavior text "deterministic stubbed triage output," which disqualifies dependence on real model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full intake orchestration in-process with stub triage and stub payer responses plus test DB; assert stage order, state propagation, and final confirmation artifact are correct.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Requires end-to-end sequence across multiple stages with at least one real agentic decision (live triage) plus external integration handoffs; failure can emerge only across the full chain. Rejected Agentic: behavior text explicitly targets full pipeline completion, not isolated triage quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run production-like intake E2E with real payer API integration (or certified sandbox), live triage model call, real scheduling DB, and confirmation dispatch path; assert automatic completion of all stages and successful final confirmation.
